title: KDNA Truth Charter (TC)
description: Locks, in authoring-time, what this judgment system is and is not before any candidate judgment is generated. Authoring-time only; never enters runtime .kdna container. See RFC-0013 §3.2.

tc_id.description: Stable identifier for this TC instance, e.g., tc_<domain>_<version>_<yyyy_mm_dd>.

domain_id.description: The KDNA domain this TC belongs to.

tc_status.description: TC lifecycle status. 'locked' is required for S6 compile under --strict-authority. 'synthesized' is what kdna build produces for legacy domains without an explicit TC.

locked_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this TC was last locked. Required when tc_status is 'locked'.

locked_by.description: Human identifier of the person who locked this TC. Required when tc_status is 'locked'.

highest_question.description: The single highest judgment question this domain is designed to answer. Used to align candidate generation in S4.

core_insight.description: One-sentence statement of what the domain's judgment system actually does. Used as a drift-prevention floor.

in_scope.description: Specific judgment questions this domain does answer. At least one entry required.

out_of_scope.description: Specific judgment questions this domain explicitly does NOT answer. At least one entry required.

highest_axiom_protected.description: The single protected axiom (often the core insight rephrased as an actionable principle). TC drift detection will check that this is not silently rewritten.

forbidden_simplifications.description: Patterns that LOOK like the domain's insight but actually drift the judgment. The TC gate checks that candidate axioms do not match these patterns.

renamed_terms.description: Term migrations that must be reflected in KDNA_Patterns.json.terminology. Each entry pairs the old term with the new term and the version effective_from. PR-3 gate uses this to warn on drift.

effective_from.description: Domain version where the rename takes effect, e.g., 4.0.0

anti_drift_rules.description: Author-confirmed rules that prevent semantic drift of the domain. These are read by the S5 Human Lock gate.

judgment_authority_holder.description: Identifier (typically an author human id) who holds final judgment authority over this domain. Required for any judgment update.
